title: limonium rumicifolium
intro: Limonium rumicifolium, also known as Sea Lavender or Ruscus-leaved Sea Lavender, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Limonium rumicifolium include treating digestive issues, such as stomach cramps and diarrhea, as well as its potential in alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, as well as being incorporated into traditional folk remedies and homeopathic treatments.
